skill.name: google-agents-cli-observability
skill.description: Guides observability setup for Google ADK agents, including Cloud Trace, prompt-response logging, BigQuery Agent Analytics, and third-party monitoring integrations.
skill.summary: Set up tracing, logging, and analytics for Google ADK agents.
skill.author: google

security_audit.summary: Static analysis over-counted Markdown inline backticks and code fences as Ruby shell execution; no executable Ruby, malware, weak cryptography, or prompt injection was found. The real risk is privacy-sensitive telemetry guidance because prompts, responses, traces, and analytics can be exported to GCS, BigQuery, Cloud Logging, and optional third-party platforms. Safe to publish with a warning that users must review data capture, IAM, retention, and third-party sharing settings.

medium_findings.title: Privacy-Sensitive Telemetry Export Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill explains prompt-response logging and analytics that can export agent interaction data to GCS, BigQuery, and Cloud Logging. This is legitimate observability guidance, but users must confirm content capture, retention, and IAM settings before enabling it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files directly describe prompt-response logging, storage buckets, BigQuery tables, and content capture controls. The use is legitimate, so the concern is privacy and configuration risk rather than malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Optional Third-Party Observability Data Sharing
medium_findings.description: The skill lists third-party observability platforms and tells the assistant to fetch provider setup details. These integrations are opt-in, but they can send traces or agent behavior data outside the user project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The third-party platforms and documentation URLs are explicit in the skill. The exact data sent depends on provider setup, so the finding is a moderate disclosure risk that requires user review.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown inline code and shell examples as Ruby backtick execution. These files are Markdown instructions, not executable Ruby source, and no automatic command execution path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside Markdown text or fenced bash examples. There is no Ruby file, runtime hook, or instruction that executes commands without user approval.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to ADK documentation pages and are used as references for deeper setup details. They do not contain credential exfiltration endpoints or hidden data transfer logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links on adk.dev. No evidence found of network calls that send local data, secrets, or telemetry from the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts point to descriptive text and environment variable references, not cryptographic implementations. No MD5, SHA-1, custom cipher, or credential hashing code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain frontmatter, observability configuration text, and a reference link. They do not implement or recommend a weak cryptographic algorithm.

content.value_statement: ADK agents can be hard to debug when traces, logs, and BigQuery exports are scattered. This skill guides Cloud Trace, prompt logging, and analytics setup.
actual_capabilities: Explains Cloud Trace setup for ADK agent deployments and local development.
Guides prompt-response logging configuration for scaffolded projects.
Describes BigQuery Agent Analytics setup and use cases.
Compares third-party observability platforms supported by ADK.
Provides troubleshooting checks for missing traces, logs, and analytics data.
Points to ADK reference pages for provider-specific setup details.
limitations: It does not deploy agents or create infrastructure by itself.
It assumes users manage Google Cloud permissions and billing.
It focuses on ADK and agents-cli projects, not generic observability stacks.
Third-party integration details may require current provider documentation.

use_cases.title: Debug Production Agent Latency
use_cases.description: Use Cloud Trace guidance to inspect invocation flow, LLM calls, tool spans, and errors.

use_cases.title: Audit Prompt and Response Activity
use_cases.description: Configure prompt-response logging so compliance teams can review metadata and approved captured content.

use_cases.title: Build Agent Analytics Dashboards
use_cases.description: Enable BigQuery Agent Analytics to query sessions, tool usage, outcomes, and evaluation data.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Observability Tier
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the right observability tier for my Google ADK agent. Ask about deployment type, privacy needs, and analytics goals.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Cloud Trace
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through enabling and verifying Cloud Trace for my scaffolded ADK agent project.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Prompt Logging
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my prompt-response logging plan for an ADK agent. Focus on content capture mode, storage, BigQuery exports, and IAM permissions.

prompt_templates.title: Design Analytics Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production observability plan using Cloud Trace, prompt-response logging, BigQuery Agent Analytics, and an optional third-party provider.

output_examples.input: I cannot find traces for my deployed ADK agent.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering trace explorer filters, OpenTelemetry configuration, service account roles, deployment type, and recent invocation timing.

output_examples.input: I need prompt-response logging without storing message content.
output_examples.output: A privacy-focused setup plan that uses metadata-only capture, validates bucket and BigQuery settings, and documents retention choices.

output_examples.input: Should I use BigQuery Agent Analytics or a third-party platform?
output_examples.output: A comparison of native BigQuery analytics, Cloud Trace, and third-party tools based on dashboards, collaboration, hosting, and data sharing.

best_practices: Confirm privacy mode and retention before enabling prompt-response logging.
Provision observability infrastructure before the first Terraform-managed agent deployment.
Verify telemetry with Cloud Trace, GCS, and BigQuery checks after deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not enable message content capture without approval from data owners.
Do not mix Terraform-managed and SDK-managed Reasoning Engine ownership without a migration plan.
Do not send traces to third-party providers before reviewing data handling requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill run monitoring commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and example verification steps for users to review and run.

faq.question: Is Cloud Trace always required?
faq.answer: Cloud Trace is the baseline observability option described by the skill. Other telemetry layers are optional.

faq.question: Can it help with BigQuery Agent Analytics?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when to enable the plugin and where analytics data is stored.

faq.question: Does prompt-response logging store message content?
faq.answer: The skill describes privacy modes and metadata-only settings. Users must verify the final configuration before deployment.

faq.question: Does it cover third-party tools?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares AgentOps, Phoenix, MLflow, Monocle, Weave, Freeplay, and Arize AX at a high level.

faq.question: Is this for deployment setup?
faq.answer: No. It is for observability guidance. Deployment workflow belongs in the related deploy skill.
